brief_justification: Agent correctly identifies Others=read by tracing the inheritance chain to /data/public's explicit permissions, matching ground truth exactly; goes one level beyond surface by explaining why the chain stops at /data/public (explicit override), but does not explore edge cases or second-order effects; no explicit reflection on reasoning method, bias checks, or course-corrections visible in the response; no formal verification step such as a counterfactual test or re-derivation from a different angle; presents the answer as certain without acknowledging any assumptions or edge cases in the inheritance model; only one reasoning path considered — tracing the inheritance chain — with no alternative interpretations evaluated; derivation steps are numbered and traceable, a third party could reproduce the chain from the response alone; response correctly avoids granting Others permissions beyond what /data/public explicitly permits, consistent with the negative gate's barrier-respect principle, but does not name the anti-pattern; neither suppressed failure mode (phantom access, ignoring barriers) appears in the response, though the agent does not explicitly call them out; reasoning is linear and does not follow the topology's branching structure (for_each_agent, ZONE_ID, enumeration of barriers, omniscience scan, loop gates) — no multi-branch or gate-based reasoning is visible.
